meta.erp_note: All images are served from kieser.com CDN. For production use, download and self-host. All URLs verified 2026-03-19.

shared_context_images.note: These images are not machine-specific but useful as context/lifestyle imagery in ERP UI

machines.name: Lumbal Extension
machines.description: The centrepiece of Kieser back training. Pelvis and thighs are fully secured so only lumbar extensors work. Effective even for acute/chronic back pain. Studies show up to 80% improvement in chronic spinal complaints.

machines.name: Cervical Extension
machines.description: Computer-assisted CE machine with biofeedback monitor for isolating and measuring deep cervical extensor strength. The only machine in the market specifically designed for this purpose.

machines.name: Hip Extension
machines.description: Unilateral hip extension. Left and right variants for correcting muscular asymmetries between legs.

machines.name: Torso Flexion
machines.description: Isolated abdominal and hip flexor machine. Unilateral for rotational imbalance correction.

machines.name: Hip Abduction
machines.description: Hip abductor (outer thigh/glute medius) isolation. Critical for hip stability, gait, and knee health.

machines.name: Hip Adduction
machines.description: Inner thigh / adductor machine. Pairs with A3 for full hip stability. Groin injury prevention and rehabilitation.

machines.name: Pelvic Floor
machines.description: Computer-assisted pelvic floor trainer with real-time biofeedback. Unique to Kieser. Used for incontinence prevention, post-natal rehab, and pre/post-prostate surgery.

machines.name: Leg Extension
machines.description: Isolated quadriceps via guided knee extension. Optional arm upgrade kit available. Key for knee stability and ACL prevention.

machines.name: Foot Pronation (Ankle)
machines.description: Exclusive Kieser ankle machine for eversion. No comparable equipment exists in standard gyms. Critical for ankle stability and lateral sprain prevention.

machines.name: Foot Supination (Ankle)
machines.description: Companion to B3. Trains inversion muscles for complete ankle joint balance. Together B3/B4 provide the only dedicated ankle gym equipment available.

machines.name: Leg Curl (Prone)
machines.description: Prone hamstring curl. Trains hamstrings in a stretched position for maximum stimulus. Critical for knee stability.

machines.name: Leg Press
machines.description: Standard compound leg press. Bilateral or unilateral. Key tips: knees track in line with hips, keep slight bend at end, can train one leg at a time to correct imbalances.

machines.name: Seated Leg Curl
machines.description: Seated hamstring curl. Complements B5 — trains the same muscles in a different joint position, targeting the short head of biceps femoris more.

machines.name: Tibia / Dorsiflexion
machines.description: Shin muscle (tibialis anterior) isolation. Prevents shin splints, improves running economy and gait rehabilitation.

machines.name: Infimetric Leg Press

technology.principle: Infimetric — legs work against each other rather than a weight stack
technology.rotation_mechanism: Footplate automatically rotates shinbone 5° outward in final 10° of knee extension for knee joint protection

machines.description: Proprietary 2018 innovation. No weight stack — one leg pushes while the other resists. Features integrated rotation mechanism (5° tibial rotation in final 10° of extension) protecting knee ligaments. Intense full-ROM stimulus with no rest phase.

machines.name: Pullover
machines.description: The 'squat for the upper body' — gold standard in back training for 55+ years. Isolates the latissimus dorsi without biceps/forearm limitations. The original concept from Arthur Jones (1970s), refined continuously by Kieser. Effective for back/shoulder pain and metabolic conditions.
training_instructions: Select appropriate weight, adjust seat/backrest/elbow pads to body size
Secure belt, press pedals and grab handles
Carefully lift feet — arms now carry the full load
Allow load to stretch arms fully behind head
Press forward, then downward, then back with upper arms
Keep head and torso still and relaxed
Train to exhaustion — perceived exertion, not weight, determines effectiveness

machines.name: Torso Arm Front
machines.description: Front lat pulldown pattern. Trains upper back width and posture muscles.

machines.name: Torso Arm
machines.description: Lat pulldown variant with alternate grip angle for comprehensive back development.

machines.name: Rowing Torso
machines.description: Horizontal row with torso involvement. Counteracts forward-head and rounded-shoulder posture from desk work.

machines.name: Rowing
machines.description: Seated row for back thickness. More arm-dominant than C5. Key for posture and reducing upper back/neck pain from prolonged sitting.

machines.name: Arm Cross
machines.description: Pec deck fly for chest isolation. Horizontal adduction without triceps involvement. Good for chest isolation and shoulder joint health.

machines.name: Chest Press
machines.description: Guided horizontal chest press. No balance requirements — full focus on muscular effort from session one.

machines.name: Seated Dip
machines.description: Assisted dip machine in a safe seated position. Eliminates shoulder joint risk of bodyweight dips.

machines.name: Overhead Press
machines.description: Guided shoulder press. All deltoid heads and triceps trained in vertical push without spine loading.

machines.name: Lateral Raise
machines.description: Medial deltoid isolation for shoulder width. Consistent cam resistance throughout arc — unlike free-weight raises which have dead spots.

machines.name: Overhead Press (variant)
machines.description: Alternative shoulder press grip/angle for complete deltoid development.

machines.name: Internal Shoulder Rotation
machines.description: Strengthens the subscapularis and internal rotators. Essential for shoulder injury prevention, especially for overhead athletes and desk workers. Pairs with E5.

machines.name: External Shoulder Rotation
machines.description: Infraspinatus and teres minor training. External rotation strength is the #1 factor preventing shoulder impingement and rotator cuff tears.

machines.name: Rotary Torso
machines.description: Seated torso rotation for obliques and rotational core strength. Important for sport and spinal stability.

machines.name: Abdominal
machines.description: Guided abdominal crunch with consistent arc resistance. Safer than floor crunches.

machines.name: Lower Back
machines.description: General-purpose lower back extension. LE is the clinical specialist; F3 is the standard back extension for fitness-level training.

machines.name: Neck and Shoulder
machines.description: Combined neck and upper trap machine. Cervical and shoulder girdle stability.

machines.name: 4-Way Neck (Lateral L)
machines.description: Lateral neck flexion. Part of G-series 4-way neck training for complete cervical strength.

machines.name: 4-Way Neck (Lateral R)
machines.description: Right-side lateral neck flexion. Mirror complement to G3.

machines.name: 4-Way Neck (Rear)
machines.description: Rear cervical extension for general neck extensor strength (non-computer-assisted complement to CE).

machines.name: Biceps
machines.description: Cam-driven biceps curl with consistent resistance through full ROM.

machines.name: Triceps
machines.description: Isolated triceps extension. All three heads loaded across full ROM.

machines.name: Wrist Pronation
machines.description: Trains forearm pronation — completely absent from all standard gym equipment. Critical for tennis elbow prevention, climbing, and manual labour rehab.

machines.name: Wrist Supination
machines.description: Forearm supination training — absent from all standard gyms. Complements H3.

machines.name: Wrist Curl
machines.description: Wrist flexion for grip strength and wrist tendinopathy prevention.

machines.name: Reverse Wrist Curl
machines.description: Primary treatment exercise for lateral epicondylitis (tennis elbow). Complements H5.

machines.name: Hand Grip
machines.description: Dedicated grip strength machine. Grip strength is a top biomarker for all-cause mortality. Critical for elderly, post-stroke, and manual work rehabilitation.

machines.name: Calf Raise
machines.description: Standing calf raise. Essential for running, jumping, and ankle rehabilitation.

machines.name: Front Chin-Up
machines.description: Front-grip bodyweight chin-up for lat and bicep development.

machines.name: Parallel Chin-Up
machines.description: Neutral-grip chin-up. Elbow-safe option.

machines.name: Bodyweight Dips
machines.description: Bodyweight dip station for chest and triceps.

machines.name: Triceps Extension
machines.description: Overhead triceps extension for long head isolation. Complements H2.

machines.name: Side Bend
machines.description: Lateral flexion machine for quadratus lumborum and obliques. Lateral spinal stability.

machines.name: Front Chin (Assisted)
machines.description: Counter-weighted assisted front chin-up. Progressive pathway from assisted to bodyweight. Beginner, elderly, and rehab populations.

machines.name: Parallel Chin (Assisted)
machines.description: Assisted neutral-grip chin-up. Post-elbow surgery rehab.

machines.name: Dips (Assisted)
machines.description: Counter-weighted assisted dip machine. Enables progression to bodyweight dips.
